name: pear-3175-flat-roughing-adaptive

machine_floor.rigidity: small-format desktop CNC (Carvera Air-class or better)

material: pear (medium hardwood, density ~700 kg/m^3, Kc ~5 N/mm^2). Most other medium hardwoods (cherry, walnut, beech) work with the same numbers; for soft wood scale feeds up ~30%, for hard wood (oak, maple) scale stepdown down ~20%.

tool.geometry_notes: Single-flute is fine too — halve chipload check. Long-flute (42mm) tools deflect; recipe leaves 0.5 mm stock for finishing to absorb the deflection.

reasoning.tolerance_mm: 0.1 mm is the standard wood-roughing balance: tighter than this just slows toolpath generation without practical benefit (finishing pass holds the real tolerance).
reasoning.maximumStepdown_mm: 1.5 mm = 47% of tool diameter. Stays well below 1xD which is the usual aggressive-but-safe ceiling for a hardwood roughing pass with a long-flute 1/8" tool. Drop to 1.0 mm for hard woods (oak, maple).
reasoning.optimalLoad_mm: 0.5 mm radial = 16% of tool diameter. Adaptive's strength is high DOC + low WOC; this keeps spindle load comfortably within the 200 W continuous budget (~38 W actual) and leaves headroom for tool dulling. Bump to 0.8 mm if your machine has 500 W+.
reasoning.fineStepdown_mm: 0.6 mm. The interleaved-fine-pass distance Fusion uses when flatAreaMachining = true. We disable flat-area so this only matters on rest passes — keep at 0.6 mm or larger.
reasoning.stockToLeave_mm: 0.5 mm. The flat finishing pass takes 0.1 mm and the 3D finish takes the remaining 0.4 mm. With a 13xD long flute the tool deflects under load — 0.5 mm absorbs it.
reasoning.flatAreaMachining: DISABLED. Adaptive is for bulk material removal — a horizontal flat-floor rework op is the right tool for flats and runs faster than adaptive's redundant fine-stepdown passes.
reasoning.boundaryMode: none. Adaptive uses the stock body's outline as its outer limit. This guarantees the 2-3 mm strip between the model bounds and the stock edge is fully roughed.
reasoning.rampType: helix. Center-cutting end-mills can plunge straight, but helix is gentler and works on non-center-cutting tools too.
reasoning.tool_feedCutting_mm_min: 430 mm/min @ 13000 RPM, 2 flutes -> chipload 0.0165 mm/tooth. Low end of pear's window (0.025-0.05). Conservative for the Carvera Air's spindle and a 13xD long tool. Bump to 600 mm/min for a more rigid machine.
reasoning.tool_spindleSpeed_RPM: 13000 RPM. The Carvera Air's spindle peaks at 15000 RPM; running near peak shortens bearing life. 13000 is a comfortable cruise.
